limit-range.name: Automate Limit Range of Summons
limit-range.hint: Limits the range of spells that include a range with them as well as shows the range limit

name-ownership.name: Add Summoner's Name to Summon's Name
name-ownership.hint: Modify the Summon's name to show who summoned it, IE 'Giorno's Goblin' etc.

effect-ownership.name: Add Summon's Owner Effect
effect-ownership.hint: Adds an effect to summons with an icon of the token that summoned them for easier tracking

rank-upgrade.name: [House Rule] Summon Spells are Rank + 1
rank-upgrade.hint: Warning: I take no responsibility for how this might impact the balance of your games. This rule treats summon spells as if they were 1 rank higher for the sake of which creatures they summon

scale-to-max-summon-level-for-rank.name: [House Rule] Summon Spells Scale to Max Level
scale-to-max-summon-level-for-rank.hint: Warning: I take no responsibility for how this might impact the balance of your games. This uses PF2e Workbench's Scale to level code to scale creatures summoned via traditional summoning spells (IE Summon Animals etc.) to the stats of a creature of the highest level that spell rank can summon.

handle-living-graveyard-movement.name: [Necromancer] Handle Living Graveyard Movement
handle-living-graveyard-movement.hint: If enabled when a Living Graveyard is moved it will send a chat message which when clicked will cause the graveyard to spawn a thrall (as in accordance with the rules)

auto-expire.name: [Necromancer] Automatically Delete Expired Thralls
auto-expire.hint: When enabled if the thrall expiration date effect expires it will automatically delete the thrall

summons.name: Refresh Summons Attacks/Saves
summons.hint: When this is active it will cause any updates to the Summoner's Spell DC (Currently only conditions) to trigger a refresh of their summons, thus updating the DC/attacks to match properly

bilocation.name: Handle Bilocation
bilocation.hint: Handles bilocation for PCs (IE clone + removal of clone upon effect removal)

thaumaturge.name: Handle Thaumaturge Mirror Implement
thaumaturge.hint: Handles thaumaturge features specifically the Mirror Implement

wooden-double.name: Handle Wooden Double's Damage
wooden-double.hint: When a Wooden Double takes damage above its max health it will roll damage equivalent to the extra damage (this damage is typeless)

token-with-art.name: [Filter] Default to Tokens with Art Only
token-with-art.hint: Have the actor picker default to showing only actors with art by default

message.name: [Reminder] Get Reminder about a Combatants's Minions
message.hint: Get a private chat reminder about a combatant's minions at the start of each round

living-graveyard.thralls: The Living Graveyard has moved. If this was from Sustain, Do you want to summon 3 new Thralls along its path?

message.wooden-double: Wooden Double's Damage Passed to the Caster

duplicate-foe.too-high: The foe is too high level for this rank of Duplicate Foe

disable-items.saved: Saved! The following Summons Assistant Automation have been disabled {list}

customization.saved: Saved the customization of the summon

bilocation.error: [Error] You don't have a bilocation clone active on this scene

mirror-implement.error: [Error] You don't have a Mirrored Self clone active on this scene

dialog.duplicate-foe: Did the target fail their save?
dialog.telekinetic-hand: Do you choose to make the hand you summon invisible?

button.title: Configure Enabled Summon Triggers
button.hint: Configure what summon sources are active

light.title: Summon a Light Orb
light.text: Do you want to summon an orb of light that's not attached to a creature? (If you select no, you'll need to instead apply the effect to the creature you want to have the light)

title.default: Choose which Mirrored Self Token to keep
title.unconscious: You're Unconscious! Choose which Mirrored Self Token stays
title.adept-action: You Shatter a Mirrored Self! Choose which one doesn't Shatter

bilocation.title: Choose which Bilocated Self Token to keep

wooden-double.place-double: Left Click to place your Wooden Double

wall.start-point: Select the start point of your wall

jagged-berms.spikes: Left Click to place Spikes ({count}), Right click to stop

red.title: Red
red.text: Deals @Damage[20[fire]] damage to anyone passing through, with a @Check[reflex|dc:resolve(@actor.system.resources.dc.value)|basic] save.
red.block: Destroys ranged weapon ammunition (such as arrows and crossbow bolts) that would pass through.

orange.title: Orange
orange.text: Deals @Damage[25[acid]] damage to anyone passing through, with a @Check[reflex|dc:resolve(@actor.system.resources.dc.value)|basic] save.
orange.block: Destroys thrown weapons that would pass through.

yellow.title: Yellow
yellow.text: Deals @Damage[30[electricity]] damage to anyone passing through, with a @Check[reflex|dc:resolve(@actor.system.resources.dc.value)|basic] save.
yellow.block: Stops acid, cold, electricity, fire, force, sonic, vitality, and void effects from passing through.

green.title: Green
green.text: Deals @Damage[10[poison]] damage to anyone passing, with a @Check[fortitude|dc:resolve(@actor.system.resources.dc.value)|basic] save and makes them @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 minute on a failure.
green.block: Stops toxins, gases, and breath weapons from passing through.

blue.title: Blue
blue.text: Creatures passing through are subject to the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.znv4ECL7ZtuiagtA]{Petrify} with a @Check[fortitude|traits:earth|dc:resolve(@actor.system.resources.dc.value)]
blue.block: Stops auditory, petrification, sonic and visual effects from passing through.

indigo.title: Indigo
indigo.text: Those passing through are subject to the effects of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.8kJbiBEjMWG4VUjs]{Warp Mind} with a @Check[will|traits:incapacitation,emotion,mental|dc:resolve(@actor.system.resources.dc.value)].
indigo.block: Stops divination and mental effects from passing through.

violet.title: Violet
violet.text: Creatures passing through must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:resolve(@actor.system.resources.dc.value)] save or they are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 minute; on a critical failure, the creature is instead sent to another plane, with the effect of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.5bTt2CvYHPvaR7QQ]{Interplanar Teleport}
violet.block: Prevents spells from targeting the other side (area effects still cross as normal).

critical-explosion.name: Critical Explosion
critical-explosion.description: Your mines explode in a cloud of shrapnel that shreds through a creature's weakest points to cause lasting harm. Add your Intelligence modifier to your mines' damage. A creature that critically fails its saving throw against one of your mines is additionally dealt persistent damage equal to your Intelligence modifier. If the mine dealt physical damage, this is persistent bleed damage. Otherwise, the persistent damage is of the same type dealt by the mine.

thrall-expiration.name: Effect: Thrall Expiration Date
thrall-expiration.description: This measures how long till this thrall expires.

rule-elements.name: Effect: Rule Elements
rule-elements.description: This effect adds various rule elements to a particular summon in order to add automation like strikes etc.

successful-save.title: Duplicate Foe - Successful Save
successful-save.text: Due to the successful save, targets only take half the damage of this duplicate's strikes
